heading: Repealed. –(3), (61), , , 88Pub. L. 93–43, § 7(a)(1)87 Stat. 82June 18, 1973
label: 271 to 276 Repealed. –(3), (61), , , 88Pub. L. 93–43, § 7(a)(1)87 Stat. 82June 18, 1973
notes: Section 271, R.S. § 4870, provided for manner of acquisition of lands.
Section 271a, , , related to State donations of land.act June 29, 1938, ch. 80852 Stat. 1233
Section 272, R.S. § 4871; , , provided for appraisement of real estate.act Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 29136 Stat. 1167
Section 273, R.S. § 4872, provided for payment of appraised value.
Section 274, R.S. § 4873, provided for superintendents of cemeteries.
Section 275, acts , ; , , , provided for selection of superintendents.Mar. 24, 1948, ch. 143, § 162 Stat. 84Pub. L. 87–17875 Stat. 411Aug. 30, 1961
Section 276, R.S. § 4875; , , provided for fuel and quarters for superintendents.act July 30, 1912, ch. 25837 Stat. 240
Subject matter is generally covered by section 2400 et seq. of Title 38, Veterans’ Benefits. See sections 2404 and 2406 of Title 38.

Effective Date of RepealRepeal effective , or such earlier date as the President may prescribe and publish in the Federal Register, see , set out as a note under , Veterans’ Benefits.Sept. 1, 1973section 10(c) of Pub. L. 93–43section 2306 of Title 38

Superintendents of National Cemeteries Under the Jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Army, , , provided that:Pub. L. 97–306, title IV, § 40596 Stat. 1443Oct. 14, 1982“Notwithstanding section 7(b)(2) of the National Cemeteries Act of 1973 () [ set out below], the provisions of the Act entitled “An Act to provide for selection of superintendents of national cemeteries from meritorious and trustworthy members of the Armed Forces who have been disabled in the line of duty for active field service”, approved [, , which enacted former ], as in effect on the day before the effective date of section 7 of the National Cemeteries Act of 1973 [see set out as a note under , Veterans’ Benefits], shall not apply with respect to the appointment of the superintendent of a national cemetery under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Army.”87 Stat. 88section 7 of Pub. L. 93–43Act Mar. 24, 1948, ch. 14362 Stat. 84section 275 of this titlesection 10(c) of Pub. L. 93–43section 2306 of Title 38March 24, 1948

Matured Rights and Duties, Incurred Penalties, Liabilities, and Forfeitures, and Commenced Proceedings Excepted in Repeal of National Cemeteries Provisions, , , provided in part that sections 271 to 276, 278 to 279d, 281 to 282, 286 to 290, and 296 of this title are repealed, except with respect to rights and duties that matured, penalties, liabilities, and forfeitures that were incurred, and proceedings that were begun before effective date of such section 7.Pub. L. 93–43, § 7(a)87 Stat. 82June 18, 1973effective , or such earlier date as the President may prescribe and publish in the Federal Register, see , set out as a note under , Veterans’ Benefits.Section 7 of Pub. L. 93–43section 10(c) of Pub. L. 93–43section 2306 of Title 38Sept. 1, 1973

Functions, Powers, and Duties of Secretaries Unaffected, , , provided that:Pub. L. 93–43, § 7(b)87 Stat. 88June 18, 1973“Nothing in this section [repealing sections 271 to 276, 278 to 279d, 281 to 282, 286 to 290, and 296 of this title and enacting provisions set out as a note under sections 271 to 276] shall be deemed to affect in any manner the functions, powers, and duties of—“(1)the Secretary of the Interior with respect to those cemeteries, memorials, or monuments under his jurisdiction on the effective date of this section [see note above], or“(2)the Secretary of the Army, the Secretary of the Navy, or the Secretary of the Air Force with respect to those cemeteries, memorials, or monuments under his jurisdiction to which the transfer provisions of section 6(a) of this Act [set out as a note under , Veterans’ Benefits] do not apply.”section 2404 of Title 38
